sourceMovie.title: The Spy Who Loved Me
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Spy Who Loved Me (1977) as a adventure theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The adventure and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Spectre
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Spy Who Loved Me clicked for you, Spectre (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Mission: Impossible - Ghost Protocol
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Spy Who Loved Me clicked for you, Mission: Impossible - Ghost Protocol (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Spy Who Loved Me?
faq.answer: Try Spectre, Mission: Impossible - Ghost Protocol, Dr. No—each shares DNA with The Spy Who Loved Me while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Spy Who Loved Me?
faq.answer: The Spy Who Loved Me (1977) is best described as Adventure, Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Spy Who Loved Me part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "James Bond Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
